qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection via WebSocket command execution endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies a command injection vulnerability in runner/parser.go via unsanitized shell input on the WebSocket /api/command endpoint. The advisory describes a path traversal vulnerability in http/public.go via filepath.Dir(link.Path) in public share links. These are different root causes, different code locations, different attack surfaces, and different vulnerability classes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a path traversal in http/public.go where filepath.Dir(link.Path) sets the BasePathFs root to the parent directory of the shared directory, allowing public share link holders to browse sibling directories. The sole qualifying finding describes a command injection in runner/parser.go where unsanitized user input is passed to a shell when the Shell setting is configured. These are distinct vulnerable code paths, distinct root causes, and distinct vulnerability classes.
evidence: Advisory root cause: filepath.Dir(link.Path) in http/public.go withHashFile sets BasePathFs to parent directory
Advisory attack surface: public share links accessible without authentication beyond knowing the link hash
Finding root cause: raw user input appended to shell command array in runner/parser.go without sanitization
Finding attack surface: authenticated WebSocket /api/command endpoint requiring execute permission and Shell setting
